title: National population estimates: At 30 June 2019 | Stats NZ
content:
## National population estimates: At 30 June 2019
15 August 2019, 10:45am
National population estimates give the best available measure of the population, by age and sex, usually living in New Zealand.
- National population estimates: At 30 June 2019 Excel spreadsheet, 29 KB

### National population estimates: At 30 June 2019
Excel spreadsheet, 29 KB
During the June 2019 year:
- New Zealand's population grew by 76,000, or 1.6 percent
- natural increase (births minus deaths) was 26,600 and net migration gain (arrivals minus departures) was 49,400.
- New Zealand’s estimated resident population was 4.92 million
- there were 2,419,700 males and 2,497,300 females
- the median age for males and females was 36.2 and 38.5 years, respectively.
New Zealand population by broad age group, 1991–2019
The national population estimates have been revised back to 30 June 2013, to incorporate the new outcomes-based migration measure (see International migration uses new official measure ). We are using interim backcast net migration estimates. These population estimates will be revised again by March 2020 to incorporate 2018 Census and 2018 post-enumeration survey results, and any updated final birth, death, and migration estimates.
After each census, we revise the intercensal population estimates to be consistent with the latest census-base estimated resident population, which implicitly corrects for any discrepancy in estimated population change. Therefore, revising population estimates before 30 June 2013 is unnecessary.

## Access data in Infoshare
Use Infoshare to view and download time-series data for annual and quarterly national population estimates by single-year of age, five-year age groups, and selected age groups. Subject category: Population Group: Population estimates – DPE

## Definitions and metadata
National population estimates – DataInfo+ gives general methodology used to produce national population estimates, and related metadata.
